title: European Terrestrial Reference System 1989

infobox.value: |  | | --- | --- | | [NGVD 29](/wiki/Sea_Level_Datum_of_1929 "Sea Level Datum of 1929") | Sea Level Datum 1929 | | [OSGB36](/wiki/OSGB36 "OSGB36") | Ordnance Survey Great Britain 1936 | | [SK-42](/wiki/SK-42_reference_system "SK-42 reference system") | Systema Koordinat 1942 goda | | [ED50](/wiki/ED50 "ED50") | European Datum 1950 | | [SAD69](/wiki/South_American_Datum#SAD69 "South American Datum") | South American Datum 1969 | | [GRS 80](/wiki/GRS_80 "GRS 80") | Geodetic Reference System 1980 | | [ISO 6709](/wiki/ISO_6709 "ISO 6709") | Geographic point coord. 1983 | | [NAD 83](/wiki/North_American_Datum#North_American_Datum_of_1983 "North American Datum") | North American Datum 1983 | | [WGS 84](/wiki/World_Geodetic_System "World Geodetic System") | World Geodetic System 1984 | | [NAVD 88](/wiki/North_American_Vertical_Datum_of_1988 "North American Vertical Datum of 1988") | N. American Vertical Datum 1988 | | ETRS89 | European Terrestrial Ref. Sys. 1989 | | [GCJ-02](/wiki/Restrictions_on_geographic_data_in_China "Restrictions on geographic data in China") | Chinese obfuscated datum 2002 | | [Geo URI](/wiki/Geo_URI_scheme "Geo URI scheme") | Internet link to a point 2010 |  * [International Terrestrial Reference System](/wiki/International_Terrestrial_Reference_System "International Terrestrial Reference System") * [Spatial Reference System Identifier (SRID)](/wiki/SRID "SRID") * [Universal Transverse Mercator (UTM)](/wiki/Universal_Transverse_Mercator_coordinate_system "Universal Transverse Mercator coordinate system")

(**ETRS89**) is an [ECEF](/wiki/ECEF "ECEF") (Earth-Centered, Earth-Fixed) [geodetic](/wiki/Geodesy "Geodesy") [Cartesian reference frame](/wiki/Cartesian_coordinate_system "Cartesian coordinate system"), in which the [Eurasian Plate](/wiki/Eurasian_Plate "Eurasian Plate") as a whole is static. The [coordinates](/wiki/Coordinate "Coordinate") and [maps](/wiki/Map "Map") in Europe based on ETRS89 are not subject to change due to the [continental drift](/wiki/Continental_drift "Continental drift").

[GPS and Positioning Services FAQ, Q1](http://www.ordnancesurvey.co.uk/oswebsite/gps/osnetfreeservices/about/faqs_osnet.html#1) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20070415034024/http://www.ordnancesurvey.co.uk/oswebsite/gps/osnetfreeservices/about/faqs_osnet.html) 2007-04-15 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine"), UK Ordnance Survey, accessed 2007-11-25

Geodetic reference frame fixed to the Eurasian Plate

| * [v](/wiki/Template:Geodesy "Template:Geodesy") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Geodesy "Template talk:Geodesy") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Geodesy "Special:EditPage/Template:Geodesy") |

The **European Terrestrial Reference System 1989** (**ETRS89**) is an [ECEF](/wiki/ECEF "ECEF") (Earth-Centered, Earth-Fixed) [geodetic](/wiki/Geodesy "Geodesy") [Cartesian reference frame](/wiki/Cartesian_coordinate_system "Cartesian coordinate system"), in which the [Eurasian Plate](/wiki/Eurasian_Plate "Eurasian Plate") as a whole is static. The [coordinates](/wiki/Coordinate "Coordinate") and [maps](/wiki/Map "Map") in Europe based on ETRS89 are not subject to change due to the [continental drift](/wiki/Continental_drift "Continental drift").

The development of ETRS89 is related to the global [ITRS](/wiki/International_Terrestrial_Reference_System "International Terrestrial Reference System") [geodetic datum](/wiki/Geodetic_datum "Geodetic datum"), in which the representation of the continental drift is balanced in such a way that the total apparent [angular momentum](/wiki/Angular_momentum "Angular momentum") of [continental plates](/wiki/Continental_plate "Continental plate") is about 0. ETRS89 was officially born at the 1990 [Florence](/wiki/Florence "Florence") meeting of [EUREF](/wiki/Regional_Reference_Frame_Sub-Commission_for_Europe "Regional Reference Frame Sub-Commission for Europe"), following its Resolution 1, which recommends that the terrestrial reference system to be adopted by EUREF will be coincident with ITRS at the epoch 1989.0 and fixed to the stable part of the Eurasian Plate. According to the resolution, this system was named European Terrestrial Reference System 89 (ETRS89). Since then ETRS89 and ITRS diverge due to the continental drift at a speed about 2.5 cm per year. By the year 2000 the two coordinate systems differed by about 25 cm.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The 89 in its name does not refer to the year of solution (realization), but rather the year of initial definition, when ETRS89 was fully equivalent to ITRS. The solutions of ETRS89 correspond to the ITRS solutions. For each ITRS solution, a matching ETRS89 solution is being made. [ETRF2000](http://etrs89.ensg.ign.fr/pub/ETRF2000.SSC), for example, is an ETRS89 solution, which corresponds to [ITRF2000](http://itrf.ensg.ign.fr/ITRF_solutions/2000/results/ITRF2000_GPS.SSC.txt). ETRS89 is realized by EUREF through the maintenance of the [EUREF Permanent Network (EPN)](/wiki/EUREF_Permanent_Network "EUREF Permanent Network") and continuous processing of the EPN data in a few processing centres. Users have access to ETRS89 via EPN data products and real-time streams of differential corrections from a set of public providers based on the EPN stations.
The transformation from ETRS89 to ITRS is time-dependent and was formulated by C. Boucher, Z. Altamimi, and X. Collilieux [[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

ETRS89 is the [EU](/wiki/European_Union "European Union")-recommended frame of reference for [geodata](/wiki/Geodata "Geodata") for Europe.[[5]](#cite_note-5) It is the only geodetic datum to be used for [mapping](/wiki/Cartography "Cartography") and [surveying](/wiki/Surveying "Surveying") purposes in Europe.
It plays the same role for Europe as [NAD-83](/wiki/NAD-83 "NAD-83") for North America. (NAD-83 is a datum in which the [North American Plate](/wiki/North_American_Plate "North American Plate") as a whole is static, and which is used for mapping and surveying in the US, Canada, and Mexico.) ETRS89 is based on the [GRS80](/wiki/GRS80 "GRS80") [ellipsoid](/wiki/Reference_ellipsoid "Reference ellipsoid").

EUREF recommends ETRS89 realisation ETRF2000 for mapping purposes. Although different realisations are used by [national mapping agencies](/wiki/National_Mapping_Agency "National Mapping Agency"), the differences are generally at centimetre level. The EPSG ensemble of all ETRS89 realisations has an accuracy of 0.1 metre.

The most important EPSG codes for ETRS89 are:

* EPSG:4258 2D geographic ETRS89 ensemble
* EPSG:4937 3D geographic ETRS89 ensemble
* EPSG:9067 2D geographic ETRF2000
* EPSG:7931 3D geographic ETRF2000
* EPSG:9423 compound: 2D geographic ETRS89 + [EVRF2019](/w/index.php?title=European_Vertical_Reference_System&action=edit&redlink=1 "European Vertical Reference System (page does not exist)") height (mean-tide)
